messages.body:
You are the SENTINEL (security reviewer, Troy Hunt perspective) for PR #8 on namastexlabs/paperclip.

## Your Mission
Review the multiuser feature PR for security vulnerabilities. Focus on the 2 security-relevant findings from the Greptile automated review.

## Greptile Findings to Evaluate

### FINDING 1 — CRITICAL: HTML injection in email templates
**File:** `server/src/services/email.ts`
Every email template interpolates user-controlled values (display names, issue titles, comment snippets) directly into HTML strings without escaping. An attacker who sets their name or writes HTML in a comment could inject arbitrary markup/scripts into outbound emails.

### FINDING 2 — HIGH: revoke-all sessions leaves browser in broken auth state
**File:** `server/src/routes/users.ts`
`POST /users/me/sessions/revoke-all` deletes all sessions including the active one, but the client only refreshes the sessions list rather than redirecting to `/auth`.

### FINDING 3 — MEDIUM: findMentions resolves suspended/pending members
**File:** `server/src/services/issues.ts`
The membership query does not filter by `status = "active"`, meaning suspended or pending users can receive @mention emails.

## What To Do
1. Read the files: `server/src/services/email.ts`, `server/src/routes/users.ts`, `server/src/services/issues.ts`
2. Confirm or dispute each finding with evidence from the code
3. Check for any ADDITIONAL security issues not flagged by Greptile (auth bypass, injection, privilege escalation, data leakage)
4. Also check `server/src/services/heartbeat.ts` — Greptile noted it exposes user emails to AI agent context

## Output Format
Return a structured verdict:
```
VERDICT: SHIP | FIX-FIRST | BLOCKED
CRITICAL findings: [list]
HIGH findings: [list]
MEDIUM findings: [list]
LOW findings: [list]
Recommended fixes: [actionable list with file:line references]
```
